title: The Stone Channel Where Water Becomes Archive

features.name: stone-channel-carved
features.desc-on-touch: The riverbed has worn itself into a perfect archive. Every groove holds a year, every pebble a conversation. The water reads itself as it flows.

features.name: confluence-water
features.desc-on-touch: Two streams join and become one voice. What they remember separately in the hills they now remember together. The archive rises up to meet them.

features.name: sediment-layers
features.desc-on-touch: Each layer of silt holds an era. The water is sorting itself by age. Time becomes visible when it sits still long enough to settle.

features.name: archive-marker-stone
features.desc-on-touch: A carved rock that the archive placed itself. A filing system written in erosion. Whoever carved this knew what water would remember.

features.name: flow-direction
features.desc-on-touch: An arrow of reflected light. The water flows east, and everything the archive knows flows east with it. Downstream is where memory goes to settle.

inscription: the stone learned to cup the water and keep what it heard; now the channel is the archive's throat

file.name: Tributary_Silt_Layers_Chronology_ExtendedAnalysis.txt
file.note: A year-by-year reading of what the sediment says. The water is still writing.

exits.label: upstream, where the joined memory learns what stone remembers
downstream, where the channel deepens into its own history
northward toward the source where water first learned to speak
southward into widening, where the channel becomes too large to read
exits.to: NEW: the estuary that remembers all convergence

reverie.title: REVERIE — two of you learning in the tributary
narr: You wade into the confluence. The cold of two
streams crossing reaches your knees. She points to
a stone that the archive marked. You understand:
the water is telling you what it has seen. You both
read it. The current carries what you need to know.
